questions.text: In the expected prediction error decomposition $\mathbb{E}[(y - \hat{f}(\mathbf{x}))^2] = \text{Bias}[\hat{f}(\mathbf{x})]^2 + \text{Var}[\hat{f}(\mathbf{x})] + \sigma^2$, which component represents the sensitivity of the model to the specific choice of the training set $\mathcal{D}$?
options: The cross-product term between predictions and noise
questions.explanation: Variance measures how much the predicted function $\hat{f}(\mathbf{x}; \mathcal{D})$ varies around its expected prediction $\mathbb{E}[\hat{f}(\mathbf{x})]$ as the training set $\mathcal{D}$ changes. High variance indicates that the model is highly sensitive to the random fluctuations and noise in the training data, leading to overfitting.

questions.text: Why is the Vapnik-Chervonenkis (VC) dimension of linear classifiers (hyperplanes) in $\mathbb{R}^2$ exactly 3, and not 4?
options: No set of 3 collinear points can be shattered by a straight line.
Any set of 4 points in a plane can be partitioned into two diagonal pairs (XOR configuration) that cannot be separated by a single straight line.
The degree of freedom for a 2D line equation is 2, limiting the VC dimension to $2^1 = 2$.
Hyperplanes in $d$ dimensions can shatter at most $2^d$ points under any spatial configuration.
questions.correct_answer: Any set of 4 points in a plane can be partitioned into two diagonal pairs (XOR configuration) that cannot be separated by a single straight line.
questions.explanation: For any set of 4 points in $\mathbb{R}^2$, they either form a convex hull or one point lies inside the triangle of the other three. In both geometric configurations, there exists a binary labeling (such as alternating vertices in a quadrilateral or labeling the inner point differently from the outer three) that cannot be separated by a linear boundary, meaning 4 points cannot be shattered.

questions.text: What is the primary conceptual advantage of using Rademacher Complexity over VC Dimension to bound a model's generalization error?
options: Rademacher complexity requires fewer samples $N$ to evaluate and is independent of the hypothesis space.
Rademacher complexity is a data-dependent measure that evaluates model capacity on the actual input distribution, whereas VC dimension is a worst-case combinatorial metric.
VC dimension bounds only apply to linear models, while Rademacher complexity only applies to neural networks.
Rademacher complexity eliminates the empirical risk term from the generalization bound, simplifying convergence checks.
questions.correct_answer: Rademacher complexity is a data-dependent measure that evaluates model capacity on the actual input distribution, whereas VC dimension is a worst-case combinatorial metric.
questions.explanation: Rademacher complexity evaluates how well the hypothesis class $\mathcal{H}$ can fit random noise on the specific coordinates of the input sample $S$. This takes into account the actual distribution of inputs. VC dimension, by contrast, is a worst-case combinatorial property that is completely independent of the data distribution, often leading to pessimistic bounds in practice.
